title: BIS warns stablecoins risk fragmenting global financial system
summary: The Basel-based institution said that private digital tokens fall short of the requirements for sound money and urged policymakers to accelerate work on tokenized forms of central bank and commercial bank money.

title: Will Bitcoin price recover in July?
summary: Bitcoin’s past July gains and heavy short bets point to a rally toward $75,000, but a break below key support keeps the risk of $55,000 in play.

title: New Zealand Dollar Faces Tough Third Quarter on Growth Headwinds
summary: New Zealand’s dollar is facing a subdued third quarter as a resurgent greenback and the lingering effects of the Iran war’s energy shock drag on the domestic recovery.

title: Bond Heavyweights Target a Market Sweet Spot for New Warsh Era
summary: Some of the biggest bond managers are zeroing in on one specific area of the market as the best place to ride out the early days of the Kevin Warsh era.

title: The Future Cyberpunk Imagined Is Here: How Much Did It Get Right?
summary: From AI to brain implants, cyberpunk got more right than many people realize. Its biggest prediction, however, wasn't the technology.

title: The Stablecoin Founder Map Doesn't Match the Stablecoin Volume Map
summary: Emerging markets drive most real-world stablecoin usage, yet founder concentration and venture funding remain U.S.- and Europe-centric.

title: Michael Saylor signals another bitcoin buy as Strategy sits about $13 billion underwater
summary: A Monday filing would mark Strategy's fourth straight week of buying, after a 520 BTC purchase on June 22 that was its smallest recent tranche.

title: SecondFi maps recovery path after $2.4 million Cardano wallet exploit, aims to return funds within two weeks
summary: An exploit drained about $2.4 million in ADA from 374 addresses over three days through a flaw in SecondFi's wallet-generation software.
